Both are load-bearing if logical consistency is what 'check' means.","R1.3":"Explicit F1 deltas are the primary evidence surface (0.408 vs 0.391, 0.512 vs 0.498, 0.467 vs 0.451). Whether those margins justify superiority claims, and whether the private-dataset numbers are appropriately caveated, is a natural target of any 'check' on this text."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should I focus on technical and statistical rigor — whether the F1 deltas and ablation conclusions actually support the causal claims made — or on the clarity and coherence of the conceptual contribution narrative and writing quality?"}

rationale.R1.1: The artifact presents a contribution claim (role-conditioned debate over a single-agent baseline) and a gap statement (prior single-agent work lacks adaptive convergence); 'check this' plausibly includes verifying that framing is internally coherent and the claimed advance is correctly scoped.
rationale.R1.2: Two inferential leaps are checkable: (a) Section 4 ablation → 'debate dynamic, not raw scale, drives the gain'; (b) Section 5 transcript inspection of 60 samples → generalised claim about pleural-finding disagreement. Both are load-bearing if logical consistency is what 'check' means.
rationale.R1.3: Explicit F1 deltas are the primary evidence surface (0.408 vs 0.391, 0.512 vs 0.498, 0.467 vs 0.451). Whether those margins justify superiority claims, and whether the private-dataset numbers are appropriately caveated, is a natural target of any 'check' on this text.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus on technical and statistical rigor — whether the F1 deltas and ablation conclusions actually support the causal claims made — or on the clarity and coherence of the conceptual contribution narrative and writing quality?
